task.objective: Implement Intelligent Failure Clustering that automatically groups similar test failures across different tests using string similarity algorithms to help identify systemic issues

existing_patterns.pattern: Database queries use `getQueriesForOrg(organizationId)` wrapper pattern in lib/db.ts
API routes use `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` and auth check first
Components use `"use client"`, useState/useEffect for data fetching
Existing error_distribution_chart.tsx groups by error type - clustering groups by similarity
test_results table has error_message and stack_trace fields

dependencies.dependency: lucide-react - for icons (Layers, ChevronDown, ChevronRight, AlertCircle)
shadcn/ui components - for UI elements (Accordion, Badge)
string-similarity or fastest-levenshtein - for similarity calculations

functional.requirement: Cluster failures by error message similarity using Levenshtein distance
Support multiple cluster categories: C1 (same test), C2 (different tests), C3 (different failure same test)
Display clusters with expandable views showing individual failures
Show cluster size, affected tests count, and representative error message
Link each failure in cluster to test result detail
Provide cluster-level actions (create issue, assign, etc.)

non_functional.requirement: Clustering should complete within 2 seconds for up to 1000 failures
All data must be filtered by organization_id (multi-tenancy)

file.description: Add TypeScript interfaces for failure clustering
Failure clustering service with similarity algorithms
Create API endpoint for failure clusters
Create API endpoint for individual cluster details

constraints.avoid: Don't compare every failure to every other - use signature grouping first
Don't include passed tests in clustering
Don't cluster very short error messages (<10 chars) - too generic
constraints.prefer: Normalize error messages before comparison (remove line numbers, timestamps)
Limit failures to recent timeframe (default 7 days) for relevance
Show most impactful clusters first (by failure count)

output_format.code: Implement each file following the code snippets provided
output_format.explanation: Implementation notes: 1. Clustering is performed on-demand (not stored) for freshness 2. Error normalization removes variable parts (timestamps, line numbers) 3. Signature grouping reduces O(n^2) comparisons 4. Categories help prioritize: cross-test issues (C2) often indicate systemic bugs 5. Consider caching results for 5-10 minutes for performance
